notes: Chunk 78 DONE (Template C, Tier B, 25 new routes, 1 block) + content plan Day 31 (South Korea country pillar, delivered as a country-guide upgrade rather than a new blog file; see below). Chunk 78 closed a reciprocal-gap cluster among five Tier B countries not yet paired both ways with the US, Australia and UAE: Uzbekistan, Brunei, Laos, Seychelles and Trinidad and Tobago. US x Uzbekistan (2 routes) + Uzbekistan-to-UK (1, completing the reciprocal pair after chunk 77 built UK-to-Uzbekistan) + UAE x {Brunei, Laos, Seychelles, Trinidad and Tobago, Uzbekistan} both directions (10) + US x {Seychelles, Trinidad and Tobago} (2, completing pairs after Seychelles/Trinidad-to-US already existed) + Australia x {Brunei, Laos, Seychelles, Trinidad and Tobago, Uzbekistan} both directions (10) = 25 routes. Researched via parallel web-search agents against primary government sources rather than reused templates, since none of these five countries had established Australia- or UAE-side data on the site before this chunk. Genuine differentiators found: Trinidad and Tobago runs a formal 4-category system on its own country list (Australia = Category 1, no titre test at all; US and UAE = Category 2, titre required) and has run a no-quarantine policy for compliant pets since April 2013, confirmed against primary Ministry of Agriculture, Land and Fisheries PDFs and the USDA's own joint export certificate for this route. Seychelles, despite being genuinely rabies-free, is NOT treated as rabies-free by either Australia (DAFF classifies it Group 3, same tier as the UK/US/most of Europe) or by its own National Biosecurity Agency for pets arriving FROM the US, Australia or UAE (its 2024 import rules only describe a rabies-affected-country pathway: titre test, fixed 3-month wait from blood draw, and a mandatory ~4-week quarantine at Seychelles' single Mahe facility); flagged an honest, unresolved inconsistency between Seychelles' 2024 procedural document and its own still-live application form over whether Australia's rabies-free status should get a lighter pathway, and the Australia-to-Seychelles page states that ambiguity directly rather than guessing. Brunei's DAA scheduled-country list (titre-exempt, quarantine-avoided, 6-month residency) names Australia, Ireland, New Zealand, Singapore and the UK by name; the UAE is not on it, so UAE-to-Brunei and US-to-Brunei both carry the full non-scheduled pathway (titre test, ~14-day facility quarantine) while Australia-to-Brunei can use the lighter scheduled pathway subject to DAA confirming residency case by case. Laos's DLF has never published which countries it treats as high-rabies-risk; rather than guess, the Australia-to-Laos and UAE-to-Laos pages state this honestly and tell the reader to confirm directly with DLF, consistent with the YMYL guard. Neither Laos nor Uzbekistan appear on any DAFF Group 1/2/3 approved-country list, so Laos-to-Australia and Uzbekistan-to-Australia both carry DAFF's non-approved-country pathway in full: the pet must live continuously in an approved third country (commonly Singapore or the UAE) for at least 180 consecutive days before Australia will process the import at all, on top of the standard RNATT test and Mickleham quarantine; both pages are marked route_complexity 'very_high' with a realistic 40-52 week timeline. Uzbekistan's own veterinary authority changed name and legal footing on 1 January 2023 (now the Committee of Veterinary and Livestock Development under the Ministry of Agriculture, not the old State Committee for Veterinary and Livestock Development still referenced on some third-party sites and possibly on an earlier UK-to-Uzbekistan sibling page); the correct current portal is vetgov.uz, not vet.uz, which returned no live response during research. CDC risk-list check (US import side): confirmed Seychelles and Trinidad and Tobago are NOT on CDC's 15 April 2026 high-risk-for-dog-rabies list (standard pathway, no titre test), while Uzbekistan IS on it (full high-risk pathway: CDC Dog Import Form, serology test, CDC-registered facility airport). All DAFF/agriculture.gov.au figures in this chunk are sourced from convergent WebSearch snippets rather than a directly-loaded primary page, since agriculture.gov.au and bicon.agriculture.gov.au returned persistent 503/403 responses to every fetch attempt across three separate research passes; flagged explicitly in-page wherever this applies, with a recommendation to verify directly via a normal browser session before treating any DAFF figure as final. All 25 files QA-scanned clean (zero em dashes, zero banned vocabulary, correct Marcus Webb persona, valid keyed-map YAML, no placeholder strings, no contact email/phone stated). Content plan Day 31: rather than create a new blog file duplicating the existing site/content/countries/south-korea.md country guide (which predates the current quality gate and contained a direct self-contradiction: import_requirements stated a mandatory 180-day post-titre wait and 10-day mandatory quarantine, while the prose overview claimed most pets clear without quarantine), rewrote that existing country guide in place with corrected, sourced APQA facts: no titre test at all for pets under 90 days; for pets 90 days or older from a non-rabies-free origin, a titre test at 0.5 IU/ml minimum, valid 24 months from the blood draw, with NO fixed post-test waiting period (unlike the EU's 90-day or Japan's 180-day rule); quarantine is not mandatory for every arrival, only an average 10-day extended hold when documentation or the titre result is non-compliant; import permit/advance notification only required for movers bringing 10 or more pets at once. Source: APQA (qia.go.kr) plus corroborating detail from seoulstart.com, cross-checked. Routes 6,371 -> 6,396, pages 7,177 -> 7,202. Chunk 79 (Template D, Tier B) is next.
_previous_notes_chunk_77: Chunk 77 DONE (Template B, Tier B, 25 new routes, 1 block) + Blog Day 30. Closed the largest gap in the Maldives cluster opened in chunk 76: all 12 remaining hub-market EU/EEA destinations not yet paired with the Maldives (Germany, France, Italy, Netherlands, Spain, Portugal, Belgium, Austria, Switzerland, Czech Republic, Poland, Hungary), both directions, plus the missing Maldives-to-Canada leg (Canada-to-Maldives already existed). Every maldives-to-X page leads with a cat (the realistic enquiry, since the Maldives' own dog-import ban has left almost no resident dog population) but correctly states that a resident dog faces no legal bar to leaving, only entering; every X-to-maldives page restates the entry ban plainly, matching the established chunk-76 framing. All 12 EU-bound pages use the current EU legal basis (Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2026/131, Implementing Regulation (EU) 2026/705, Implementing Regulation (EU) 2026/636), reusing verified per-destination facts from sibling Lebanon-to-EU route files rather than re-deriving them. Switzerland (both directions) is a genuine open question handled honestly: FSVO only requires the titre test, 3-month wait and advance permission for pets from countries it classifies as high rabies risk, and we could not confirm the Maldives' specific listing on FSVO's own country list despite the Maldives being rabies-free/low-risk under comparable UK and US classifications, so both Switzerland pages state the ambiguity directly and give a wide 8-26 week range rather than a false single estimate. Maldives-to-Canada: verified CFIA's actual personal-pet-cat rule is markedly lighter than the EU/UK pathway, a rabies vaccination certificate only, no titre test, no 90-day wait, no import permit. Genuine differentiating facts verified per EU destination via web search: Austrian Airlines' non-stop Vienna-Male service (the only Gulf-free EU routing found); Edelweiss Air's seasonal direct Zurich-Male service; LOT Polish Airlines' scheduled direct Warsaw-Male service; Neos' seasonal direct Milan Malpensa-Male and Prague-Male charter services; Condor's seasonal Frankfurt-Male service; French Bee's newly announced Paris Orly-Male route launching 19 December 2026; the Air France-KLM-Martinair Cargo Animal Hotel at Schiphol handling NVWA border inspection on-site; Madrid-Barajas' four separate live-animal Border Control Posts across three ground handlers; Lisbon's Terminal 6 live-animal freight handled by CEAL Cargo; and, where no direct flight could be confirmed (Belgium, Hungary), Male's lack of a resident pet-shipping agent, with most bookings routed through IPATA-certified agents in Colombo, Sri Lanka. All 25 files QA-scanned clean (zero em dashes, zero banned vocabulary, valid keyed-map YAML for every requirements block, correct Marcus Webb persona, no placeholder strings, no contact email/phone stated). Blog Day 30: iata-pet-crate-guide.md (new article, Marcus Webb, approximately 2,800 words), covering IATA crate sizing and construction rules. Caught and corrected a genuine, widespread site error while researching: the current IATA Live Animals Regulations (52nd edition, January 2026) place domestic dogs and cats under Container Requirement 1, not Container Requirement 82 as commonly assumed across the pet transport industry, including some existing pages on this site; CR82 actually governs wild canid/felid species (wolves, jackals, foxes, bobcats, lynx). Kept 'CR82' in the title/keywords for search intent (it is the term the market searches), but the body states the correct CR1 attribution honestly. Also corrected two other common errors: ventilation must cover all four sides at a minimum 16% of combined surface area (some existing site pages say three sides), and cable ties are permitted as a supplementary fastener, not banned outright, only forbidden as a sole/primary fastening method. Flagged below for a future correction pass: existing site pages that state 'CR82' or 'three-sided ventilation' for domestic pet crates should be corrected to match this research. Routes 6,346 -> 6,371, blog 431 -> 432, content plan articles 29 -> 30, pages 7,151 -> 7,177. Chunk 78 (Template C, Tier B) is next.

counts.note: total_site_pages counts content .md source files. The full deployed total (including Hugo taxonomy and list pages) is verified from sitemap.xml after a build.

counts_method: Reconciled from disk by verify_build_state.py. Never hand-edit count fields; run 'python verify_build_state.py --write' instead.
